task: Stage-1 MMaDA-8B coarse (4x4) self-selection vs direct (32x32) self-selection vs baseline

selector_grounding.comment: Across all error iterations, how often MMaDA's free-form 4x4 letter-cell localization (A1..D4) produced usable cells vs falling back to per-token confidence pooling.
selector_grounding.contrast_direct_32x32: At the 32x32 grid (Phase 8) MMaDA could essentially never ground free-form R{row}C{col} cells (~0%), so the direct arm ran almost entirely on the confidence fallback. At 4x4 it grounds ~39% of the time, i.e. the coarse grid is materially more tractable for MMaDA's MMU.

conclusion: On final image quality the coarse-4x4 and direct-32x32 self-selection arms are tied (both 51.6% clean, both 0/0/64 ties vs baseline). The decisive difference is in the SELECTOR: at 4x4 MMaDA actually grounds its own understanding into grid cells ~39% of the time, whereas at 32x32 it does not, so the original ASCR coarse-then-dilate strategy is the more faithful and more accurate selector when MMaDA judges itself.
